post.summary: Ask a question. Returns an answer with citations to source files and locators.

topK.description: Optional source cap before prompt planning.

sessionId.description: Optional session ID for multi-turn conversation context.

driveUrl.description: Direct browser URL for opening the Drive file.

sessionId.description: Session ID if a session was used, null otherwise.

404.description: Session not found (when sessionId is provided but invalid).

post.summary: Create a new Ask conversation session.

title.description: Optional display title for the session.

projectId.description: Optional project scope. All queries in this session default to this project.

delete.summary: Delete an Ask conversation session and all its history.

get.summary: Check auth state and confirm yantramate folder is visible
Redirect to Google's OAuth consent screen
OAuth callback. Exchanges code for tokens and persists them to disk.
List top-level folders under yantramate (each = one project)
Recursively list all files in a project folder

200.description: All files inside the project subtree

get.summary: Recursively list all files in a project folder, resolved by folder name

200.description: All files inside the project subtree

404.description: No top-level project folder with that name

get.summary: Get metadata for a single file
Live-fetch the current content of a file from Drive
get.description:
Returns plain text for Google Docs/Sheets/Slides (exported on the fly) and base64 bytes for binary files.
This is the **freshness path** — every call hits Drive directly, never the cache/index.

post.summary: One-time full crawl of yantramate and capture initial Changes API cursor

200.description: Summary of files walked + new page token

post.summary: Run a delta sync — return everything that changed since the stored cursor

get.summary: Show the current Changes API cursor and last sync timestamp
Hierarchical view of the yantramate root annotated with index state
get.description:
Walks the Drive root subtree and joins it with what's in the vector DB.
Each file node carries `indexed`, `chunkCount`, `supported`, and `lastIndexedAt`
so you can immediately spot files Drive has but the index doesn't (e.g.
unsupported MIME, extraction error, not yet ingested).

post.summary: Chat with the AI assistant over your indexed mailbox.
post.description:
Tool-calling chat backed by the locally-stored email index (Postgres + pgvector,
kept fresh by the IMAP IDLE watcher). The assistant can list recent mail,
summarise time windows, semantic-search by topic, and pull a full body by id.

**Sessions**: pass `sessionId` (from `POST /email/chat/session`) to use server-side
conversation history. The server loads prior turns automatically and persists each
new exchange, so clients should not send chat history.

message.example: What's new in my mail today?

sessionId.description: UUID from POST /email/chat/session. Enables server-side history.

post.description:
Creates a new server-side chat session for the given account. Returns a `sessionId` UUID
that can be passed to `POST /email/chat` for persistent conversation history.

accountUser.description: Email account whose indexed mailbox this session belongs to.

title.description: Optional display title for the session.

post.summary: List chat sessions for an account
post.description:
Returns all chat sessions for the resolved account, ordered by most recently updated.
Messages are not included — use `POST /email/chat` with the sessionId to continue.

accountUser.description: Email account whose sessions should be listed.

delete.description: Permanently deletes the chat session and all its stored messages.

get.summary: List emails with Gmail-style filters (sender, subject, date range, seen, flagged, has-attachment, full-text)
get.description:
Returns recent emails newest-first, with optional filtering and inclusion of body / attachment metadata.
This endpoint is query-param based so it can be tested directly from Swagger.

**Filtering** uses IMAP server-side SEARCH where possible (fast). The `hasAttachment` filter
requires fetching body structures and is heavier. Combine it with other filters to narrow first.

**Pagination** uses `page` (1-based) and `limit` over the filtered UID set.

**Body / attachments** are *not* fetched by default. Opt in with `includeBody` and/or `includeAttachments`.
For attachment content (base64), use `GET /api/v1/email/get`.

A JSON-body POST variant is also accepted at the same path for clients that should not put IMAP credentials
in a URL.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Free-text search across subject and body.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Only emails on/after this date. Accepts ISO date or datetime.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Only emails before this date. Accepts ISO date or datetime.

parameters.name: seen
parameters.description: true = read only, false = unread only, omit = both.

parameters.name: flagged
parameters.description: true = starred/flagged only, false = unflagged only.

parameters.name: hasAttachment
parameters.description: true = only emails with attachments, false = only without.

parameters.name: includeBody
parameters.description: Include parsed text + HTML body in each item.

parameters.name: includeAttachments
parameters.description: Include attachment metadata. Content not included.

200.description: Filtered, paginated list of messages (newest UID first).

get.summary: Fetch one email by UID with full body (text + html) and attachments
get.description:
Returns the complete parsed email: text body, HTML body, headers (cc/bcc/reply-to/message-id),
and attachment metadata. Set `includeAttachmentContent=true` to also receive each attachment's
base64-encoded content (useful for download / preview).

A JSON-body POST variant is also accepted at the same path for clients that should not put IMAP credentials
in a URL.

parameters.name: includeAttachmentContent
parameters.description: Include base64 content for each attachment.

404.description: Email not found at that UID in the mailbox

post.summary: Manually sync the mailbox to fetch missed emails
post.description:
Connects to the IMAP server and ingests any emails that are not currently stored in the vector database.
Useful when the background IDLE watcher was disconnected or missed events.

**Use `connectionId`** — the UUID returned by `POST /email/credentials`. The background IDLE listener
handles new mail automatically; call this endpoint only to backfill missed emails.

If `ingested: 0` and `totalPending: 0`, the database is already fully in sync.

connectionId.description: UUID returned by POST /email/credentials. Use this instead of raw credentials.

limit.description: Max emails to ingest in this batch. Increase to sync more at once.

ingested.description: Number of new emails ingested in this batch

totalPending.description: Total emails missing before this sync ran

post.description:
Saves IMAP connection details in the database and returns a UUID `connectionId`.
This UUID can be used in the `POST /email/sync` and `POST /email/chat` endpoints.
The backend will immediately hot-start a background IDLE listener for this account.

**Duplicate check**: If the same email address is already registered, returns `409 Conflict`
without re-verifying credentials or re-embedding.

message.example: Email you@example.com is already registered. Remove it first before re-adding.

get.description: Returns all registered IMAP credentials. Passwords are stripped from the response.

delete.summary: Delete a registered email by its address
delete.description: Removes the IMAP credential record for the given email address.

parameters.name: emailAddress
parameters.description: The email address (IMAP user) to unregister.

404.description: No credential found for that email address

get.summary: List all projects mirrored from Drive
List indexed files for a project

content.description: Plain text for Docs/Sheets/Slides. Base64 for binary files (PDF, image, docx).

label.description: Optional display label for a saved credential.

content.description: Base64-encoded content. Only present when requested.

attachments.description: Only present when includeAttachments=true (metadata, no content).

tags.name: Ask
tags.description: Question answering over indexed Drive content.

tags.name: Drive
tags.description: Google Drive read-only access. OAuth tokens are persisted locally so re-auth is not needed between restarts.

tags.name: Email
tags.description: IMAP email access, indexing, chat, sessions, and saved connection management.

tags.name: Projects
